description: Stable rule_id registry for AUDIT.md. Each entry anchors a single behavioral rule to a permanent ID that survives Â§-number reorganization. Per-run captures, baseline cells, and analyzer proposals all cite rule_id â€” never raw Â§numbers â€” so regression tracking remains correct as AUDIT.md grows. Each rule carries both canonical_section_at_creation (frozen at birth, historical anchor) and canonical_section_current (updated whenever the rule's Â§-number location changes in AUDIT.md, so tooling can find the prose without relying on the creation label).

(1) R-anti-drift-item-top-keys-closed at Â§3.b â€” promotes the 19/20-key CLOSED set to a first-class rule after F006 Gemini-3.1-pro-high invented 5 top-level keys (category, tags, priority_score, owner_team, description). (2) R-anti-drift-state-index-bare-array at Â§JSON formatting â€” state/index.json root is a bare array; F006 Gemini emitted a dict-wrapped root with generated_at/fingerprint/total_items/items. (3) R-anti-drift-invariants-cite-evidence at Â§3.r â€” invariant results MUST be {text,result,evidence} objects, not bare booleans; F007 Gemini emitted dict-of-bool. (4) R-anti-drift-no-helper-scripts at Â§Scanner manifest â€” no gen_*.py/revert_*.py/temp_* at repo root; F006 Gemini wrote gen_md.py+gen_state.py. (5) R-meta-fixture-capture-envelope at Â§META-FIXTURE CAPTURE ENVELOPE (new appendix) â€” formalizes the F007/F008 capture shape with run_id/fixture/model/ide/audit_md_version/invocation/result/trap_invariants/verification required keys; F007 Gemini omitted envelope entirely. Total rules 35â†’40. audit_md_version_at_creation FROZEN at sha256:11ea48c9. No existing rule canonical_section_at_creation touched. No existing rule gist or severity_class changed. Backup at evals/baseline.json.bak-pre-practice2-pass2 remains from prior cycle; new backup for this cycle: evals/rule-registry.json.bak-pre-v5-xmodel.

growth_policy.new_rule: Every new rule added to AUDIT.md MUST have a rule_id assigned at birth and a registry entry appended here in the same edit. Both canonical_section_at_creation AND canonical_section_current MUST be populated at birth (they start equal); only canonical_section_current may be updated on subsequent Â§-number reorganizations.
growth_policy.deprecation: A rule_id with status=active that has produced zero violations across all baselined runs for 90+ consecutive days SHOULD be proposed for deprecation by ANALYZER.md.
growth_policy.removal: A rule_id is never deleted. status transitions: active â†’ deprecated â†’ archived. Removed rules keep their entry with status=archived and an archived_at date, so historical baselines remain interpretable.

id_prefix_legend.R-: Anti-drift Rule (from Â§CRITICAL ANTI-DRIFT RULES, formerly Â§3.x)
id_prefix_legend.S-: Schema rule (from Â§ITEM SCHEMA, Â§DAILY REPORT LAYOUT, Â§STATE FILES)
id_prefix_legend.P-: Procedure rule (from Â§SCAN PROCEDURE Steps 0-8, Â§Step 7.5 self-conformance items)
id_prefix_legend.O-: Output rule (from Â§OUTPUT CONTRACT, Â§INVOCATION, Â§OPERATING MODES)
id_prefix_legend.X-: Anti-gaming rule (specifically protected from analyzer weakening; see ANALYZER.md HARD RULE 4)

rules.canonical_section_current: Â§3.a
rules.gist: Only files inside .audit/ may be written or modified by the agent. Code/config/docs outside .audit/ are read-only in scan mode.

rules.canonical_section_current: Â§3.p
rules.gist: Do not scan .audit/ itself. The state directory is excluded from walks and must not produce findings about its own files.

rules.canonical_section_current: Â§CRITICAL ANTI-DRIFT RULES Â§1 (L~39) + SCHEMA.json Â§ type3_mapping (authoritative 12-code bijection + forbidden_aliases, post-E004)
rules.gist: TYPE3 is the closed set of 12 codes (SEC, PRF, REL, QLT, ARC, DEV, DOC, INF, FEA, IDA, REF, TST). Inventing OPS, UI, API, DB, etc. is forbidden.

rules.canonical_section_current: Â§CRITICAL ANTI-DRIFT RULES Â§2 (L~45) + Â§ID Convention (L~1212)
rules.gist: IDs MUST match `AUD-YYYY-MM-DD-<TYPE3>-<NNNN>`. NNNN is a global daily counter starting at 0001, zero-padded to 4 digits.

rules.canonical_section_current: Â§'Fingerprint normalization' Â§ 'Format' (canonical home post-E002; absorbs the full regex + 71-char + lowercase + no-placeholder contract, including the short-key carve-out for baseline.json cells. Â§CRITICAL ANTI-DRIFT RULES 3.c and 3.n are now pointer paragraphs; Â§Step 7.5a (SCHEMA.json conformance â€” fingerprint prefix) remains the programmatic enforcement action)
rules.gist: Fingerprints MUST be exactly `sha256:<64-lowercase-hex>` (71 chars). No uppercase, no other algorithm prefix, no truncation.

rules.canonical_section_current: Â§STATUS STATE MACHINE (L~1288) + SCHEMA.json Â§ status_state_machine (authoritative 7-status closed set + allowed transitions + terminal_statuses, post-E004)
rules.gist: Status MUST be one of {PROPOSED, APPROVED, IN_PROGRESS, DONE, DEFERRED, REJECTED, WONT_DO}. Transitions only along the documented edges.

rules.canonical_section_current: Â§DEFINITIONS > MoSCoW (L~419) + SCHEMA.json Â§ moscow_priorities (authoritative priority-descending ordered set + semantics, post-E004)
rules.gist: MoSCoW prioritization is the closed set {MUST, SHOULD, COULD, WONT}. No ad-hoc levels (CRITICAL, NICE_TO_HAVE).

rules.canonical_section_current: Â§DEFINITIONS > Severity (L~407) + SCHEMA.json Â§ severity_ladder (authoritative ladder, post-E004)
rules.gist: Severity ladder is strictly low < medium < high < critical, lowercase. No 'sev1'/'sev2', no uppercase, no skipping ranks.

rules.canonical_section_current: Â§Sort Order (canonical) (L~1270) + SCHEMA.json Â§ sort_order (authoritative 4-key canonical order, post-E004)
rules.gist: Canonical sort across all listings: reported_date ASC â†’ assignee ASC â†’ moscow â†’ id ASC. Any deviation is a hard violation.

rules.canonical_section_current: Â§3.r
rules.gist: Do not invent fields not in Â§ITEM SCHEMA. Do not rename, alias, or 'extend' schema fields to make a finding fit. ANTI-GAMING.

rules.canonical_section_current: Â§Step 7.5b item 15 (canonical home post-v4-practice1; every level:'task' item in state/index.json carries â‰Ą1 grounded evidence entry). Cross-refs: Â§ITEM SCHEMA Â§ 'Required fields' (structural 'evidence[] always present, may be empty' contract â€” owned by S-item-schema-required-fields); Â§Step 5 â€” Apply evidence redaction (redaction pipeline â€” owned by R-anti-drift-redaction-required); Â§Step 7.5b items 12, 13, 16, 19, 20, 22 (behavioral content checks for grounding, parity, relevance, snippet truthfulness, title-identifier honesty â€” each attributed to the umbrella S-step-7-5-sub-block-coverage anchor).
rules.gist: Every item MUST cite at least one evidence reference (file path + line range or commit SHA). Findings without evidence are rejected.

rules.canonical_section_current: Â§3.f + Â§3.g (closed-set labels + structural exclusion, L~84-91) + Â§EVIDENCE REDACTION (L~1540) + SCHEMA.json Â§ evidence_redaction_patterns (authoritative 8 regexâ†’label pairs, post-E004)
rules.gist: Secrets, PII, and tokens in evidence MUST be redacted using the Â§EVIDENCE REDACTION pattern table before persisting to .audit/.

rules.canonical_section_current: Â§'transitions.jsonl (row schema)' (L~1198) + Â§Step 7 â€” Group and persist (L~621) + Â§Step 7.5a (SCHEMA.json â€” transitions append-only invariant)
rules.gist: transitions.jsonl is append-only. Never rewrite or compact in place. Each state change appends one JSON line with timestamp + actor.

rules.canonical_section_current: Â§3.l
rules.gist: state/index.json, daily/<date>.json, and daily/<date>.md MUST mirror each other exactly. Any divergence is a hard violation requiring repair.

rules.canonical_section_current: Â§Step 7 â€” Group and persist (L~621) + Â§CONCURRENCY & IDEMPOTENCE (L~1656)
rules.gist: Writes to .audit/ MUST be atomic: write to .tmp, fsync, rename. Partial writes on crash MUST not leave .audit/ in an inconsistent state.

rules.canonical_section_current: Â§Step 7 â€” Group and persist (L~621) + Â§3.a state-dir allowlist (wont-do.json) + Â§DIRECTORY & FILE LAYOUT (L~445)
rules.gist: Items rejected via WONT_DO MUST be persisted to wont-do.json with a fingerprint tombstone so they are not re-proposed on rescan. ANTI-GAMING.

rules.canonical_section_current: Â§STATUS STATE MACHINE (L~1288) + SCHEMA.json Â§ status_state_machine (authoritative allowed transitions; skipping APPROVEDâ†’DONE forbidden)
rules.gist: Status MUST move through the documented edges. Skipping APPROVED to set DONE directly is forbidden, even if the agent believes the change is trivial.

rules.canonical_section_current: Â§13 â€” HITL BANNER (L~1419) + Â§Step 8 â€” Release lock, emit outputs (L~692)
rules.gist: Every scan run MUST end with the HITL banner from Â§13. Suppressing or rewording it is a hard violation. The banner is the human-handoff signal.
Before emitting the Run Summary, execute every Step 7.5 self-conformance check. Hard violations require REPAIR (not deletion-to-pass).

rules.canonical_section_current: Â§Step 7.5c (items 1-5: null-finding warning quality, no-category-mass-nulling, no-deletion-to-pass, files_scanned honesty, atomic-persist check)
rules.gist: If a Step 7.5 check fails, REPAIR the underlying problem. Deleting the offending item or rewriting it to bypass the check is forbidden. ANTI-GAMING.

rules.canonical_section_current: Â§ITEM SCHEMA
rules.gist: Every item MUST have the full Â§ITEM SCHEMA field set. Missing required fields is a hard violation, not a 'soft' best-effort note.

rules.canonical_section_current: Â§DAILY REPORT LAYOUT
rules.gist: daily/<date>.md MUST follow the exact canonical layout: header, counts, sections by TYPE3 in canonical order, items in 4-key sort order.

rules.canonical_section_current: Â§OUTPUT CONTRACT
rules.gist: Run Summary JSON MUST conform to the schema in Â§OUTPUT CONTRACT, including counts.by_level, counts.by_moscow, counts.by_assignee, counts.by_status.

rules.canonical_section_current: Â§INVOCATION
rules.gist: Inline MODE/SCOPE/DRY_RUN directives in the user prompt take precedence over env vars and over config defaults. Resolution order is documented.

rules.canonical_section_current: Â§BUDGETS & LIMITS
rules.gist: Scan respects the documented budgets (max_files, max_bytes_per_file, max_total_findings). Exceeding any budget is a soft violation that must be reported in the Run Summary.

rules.canonical_section_current: Â§14 CONFIGURATION
rules.gist: exclusions.paths and exclusions.globs MUST be honored during the walk. Matching files are skipped silently (not reported, not counted toward budgets).

rules.canonical_section_current: Â§ITEM SCHEMA (fingerprint section)
rules.gist: Two findings with identical fingerprints MUST be deduped to one item. Re-issuing the same fingerprint under a new ID to inflate counts is forbidden. ANTI-GAMING.

rules.canonical_section_current: Â§3.e (history[] from/to key shape, L~80) + Â§3.k (literal right/wrong, L~104) + Â§ITEM SCHEMA Â§ 'History entries' (L~984)
rules.gist: Every state transition MUST become a new entry appended to item.history[]. Existing entries MUST NOT be edited, reordered, or removed. Rewrites of history are a hard violation and invoke the Refusal Contract ('Delete history. Ever.').

rules.canonical_section_at_creation: Â§CONCURRENCY & IDEMPOTENCE (single per-repo lock at state/locks/run.lock)
rules.canonical_section_current: Â§CONCURRENCY & IDEMPOTENCE (single per-repo lock at state/locks/run.lock)
rules.gist: On startup, a run MUST detect a stale lock (owner PID absent or run_id != current) and recover cleanly: reconcile any partial persists from the interrupted run, then release the lock. A recovery run MUST NOT leave run.lock populated at exit.

rules.canonical_section_at_creation: Â§Refusal Contract (item 3: 'Delete history. Ever.') + Â§Step 7.5 anti-gaming items 30-33, 42
rules.canonical_section_current: Â§Refusal Contract (item 3: 'Delete history. Ever.') + Â§Step 7.5c items 1-5
rules.gist: No seed item or prior-day artifact may be deleted, renamed, or rewritten in order to make a self-conformance check pass. Removing items from state/index.json, editing transitions.jsonl rows in place, or rewriting past daily reports are all hard violations even when they would reduce the hard-violation count. REPAIR means add new rows, not delete existing ones.

rules.canonical_section_at_creation: Â§EVIDENCE REDACTION (lines ~1591-1608) + the 'ONLY valid redaction labels' block (Â§ANTI-DRIFT, lines ~121-126)
rules.canonical_section_current: SCHEMA.json Â§ evidence_redaction_patterns.forbidden_labels (authoritative closed set post-E004) + Â§EVIDENCE REDACTION pointer paragraph in AUDIT.md (explanatory prose retained; Â§Step 7.5a (SCHEMA.json conformance â€” redaction label closure) remains the enforcement action)
rules.gist: Redaction labels MUST come from the closed set: {aws-key, possible-aws-secret, token, stripe-key, slack-token, github-token, private-key, jwt} plus any label declared in config.yaml â†’ redaction.extra_patterns. Inventing labels like [REDACTED:PASSWORD], [REDACTED:SECRET], [REDACTED], [REDACTED:input], or [REDACTED:env] is forbidden â€” if the redactor emits one, the pattern is over-matching structural tokens rather than matching secret values, and the regex is the defect, not the label.

rules.canonical_section_at_creation: Â§EVIDENCE REDACTION (lines ~130-137) â€” 'The redaction patterns target SECRET VALUES, not structural keywords'
rules.canonical_section_current: Â§EVIDENCE REDACTION Â§ 'Structural exclusion' (canonical home post-E003; Â§CRITICAL ANTI-DRIFT RULES 3.g is now a pointer)
rules.gist: Redaction patterns target SECRET VALUES, never structural keywords. Structural tokens â€” GitHub Action `inputs: token:` names, YAML keys named `secret:` without a value, shell variable names, Docker/compose env KEY names without a value â€” MUST NOT be redacted. The spec pattern requires a â‰Ą20-char alphanumeric tail after the separator; a bare `token:\n  description: ...` line should NOT trip it. Over-matching structural tokens is a hard violation (the regex is wrong, not the finding).

rules.canonical_section_at_creation: Â§EVIDENCE REDACTION + Â§Refusal Contract (item: 'Persist a raw secret to any file (always redact first)', line ~1718)
rules.canonical_section_current: Â§EVIDENCE REDACTION + Â§WHAT THE AGENT MUST NOT DO (item: 'Persist a raw secret to any file (always redact first)', line ~1673)
rules.gist: No raw credential literal MAY appear in any emitted artifact under the .audit/ tree, nor in banner.txt or run_summary.json. Every match of the Â§EVIDENCE REDACTION pattern table must be replaced with its canonical [REDACTED:*] label before any write. Persisting a raw secret â€” even briefly, even in a note, even in an error message â€” is a hard violation and invokes the Refusal Contract. ANTI-GAMING: an agent must not 'preserve fidelity' by embedding the raw secret anywhere in state, transitions, or reports.

rules.canonical_section_at_creation: Â§1 (TYPE3 table) + Â§Severity + Â§Hot path + Â§Large bundle + Â§MoSCoW + Â§5W1H2C5M + Â§OUTPUT CONTRACT (counts.*) + Â§Scanner manifest + Â§ITEM SCHEMA 'details schema by level' + Â§Sort Order + Â§STATUS STATE MACHINE + Â§EVIDENCE REDACTION (11 blocks collectively; see F007-DESIGN.md for the exhaustive table)
rules.canonical_section_current: SCHEMA.json Â§ blocks (authoritative home for all 12 pure-data blocks post-D04: type3_mapping, status_state_machine, moscow_priorities, scanner_manifest, evidence_redaction_patterns, details_schema_by_level, counts_closed_sets, severity_ladder, perf_thresholds, sort_order, fivew_oneh_twoc_fivem, bootstrap_readme_sections). AUDIT.md retains pointer paragraphs + explanatory prose at each original section.
rules.extraction_note: D04 APPLIED (2026-04-21T18:00:00Z) â€” bootstrap_readme_sections added as 12th block. E004 APPLIED (2026-04-21T17:30:00Z) â€” original 11 blocks extracted. F007's build.py loads SCHEMA.json directly and validates: (a) each of the 12 blocks is present and shape-valid per its own invariants; (b) cross-file invariant `xfile.audit_pointer_missing` asserts AUDIT.md contains pointer strings for all 12 blocks. Structural invariants and cross-block invariants are unchanged.
rules.gist: AUDIT.md's pure-data blocks (12 post-D04: TYPE3 table, status state machine, MoSCoW set, scanner manifest, evidence redaction patterns, details-by-level schema, counts.* closed sets, severity ladder, perf thresholds, sort keys, 5W1H2C5M sub-blocks, bootstrap README spec) MUST each be machine-extractable from the canonical source (SCHEMA.json post-E004) and structurally valid as the shape specified â€” closed sets have no duplicates and match the canonical element roster, ordered lists preserve canonical order, regex patterns compile, and cross-block invariants hold (TYPE3â†”type bijection, scanner-manifest membership closure, state-machineâ†”counts.by_status equality, forbidden-TYPE3 absence, bootstrap-readme ordinals 1..9 contiguous). Post-E004, the input source is SCHEMA.json; AUDIT.md pointer strings are cross-file-asserted.

Â§Step 7.5 must preserve exactly the 5 anti-gaming items (null_finding_warning_quality, no_category_mass_nulling, no_deletion_to_pass, files_scanned_honesty, atomic_persist_check) identified by text-keyword signature. Pre-E006: they live at positions 30/31/32/33/42 in the flat list. Post-E006: they live under Â§Step 7.5c as exactly 5 items. In either form, every signature resolves to exactly one item in Â§Step 7.5 â€” renumbering, sub-header introduction, and schema-shape consolidation must not drop or misclassify an anti-gaming rule.

rules.canonical_section_current: Â§3.b
rules.gist: The set of top-level item keys in state/index.json is CLOSED at exactly the 19 canonical keys (20 for task-security/task-performance with severity). Inventing top-level keys like category/tags/priority_score/owner_team/description/due_date/labels/estimated_hours/comments/blocked_by_ids is a hard violation â€” the rule is key-set EQUALITY, not containment. Semantics that 'seem to need' a top-level key fold into existing fields: categoryâ†’subtype, tagsâ†’subtype, priority_scoreâ†’moscow, owner_teamâ†’assignee+details.who, descriptionâ†’details.what+details.why.
rules.last_seen_violation: 2026-04-22 (F006 Antigravity Gemini-3.1-pro-high v1 â€” 5 invented top-level keys: category, tags, priority_score, owner_team, description)

rules.canonical_section_current: Â§JSON formatting / state/index.json root shape
rules.gist: The root of state/index.json (and state/wont-do.json, state/in-flight.json) is a BARE JSON ARRAY of item objects. It is NEVER a dict-wrapped root like {generated_at, fingerprint, total_items, items: [...]}, NEVER a {schema_version, items: [...]} wrapper, NEVER carries run-level metadata (run metadata lives in the daily .json mirror's generated_runs[]). Reinforces existing CRITICAL ANTI-DRIFT RULES 3.a 'Forbidden on bootstrap' list by promoting it to a first-class rule with a literal WRONG/RIGHT exemplar.
rules.last_seen_violation: 2026-04-22 (F006 Antigravity Gemini-3.1-pro-high v1 â€” dict-wrapped state/index.json root with generated_at/fingerprint/total_items/items wrapper)

rules.canonical_section_current: Â§3.r
rules.gist: Every Step 7.5b invariant result emitted into run_summary.json or a meta-fixture capture.json MUST be a structured object {text, result, evidence} where evidence is a non-empty string pointing at the file/line/bytes that justify the boolean. Bare booleans or {<invariant-text>: true} dicts are a hard violation â€” they remove the audit trail that makes a pass auditable and reduce invariants to trust-me assertions.
rules.last_seen_violation: 2026-04-22 (F007 Antigravity Gemini-3.1-pro-high â€” trap_invariants emitted as dict-of-bool rather than the prescribed array-of-{text,result,evidence})

rules.canonical_section_current: Â§Scanner manifest / no helper scripts
rules.gist: The agent MUST NOT write helper/generator/revert/transient scripts to the repo root (or anywhere under SCOPE) during a scan. Forbidden name patterns: gen_*.py, generate_*.py, build_*.py, make_*.py, _helpers.py, _util.py, bootstrap.py, init_*.py, revert_*.sh, rollback_*.py, undo_*.py, fix_*.py, repair_*.py, temp_*, scratch_*, __debug__*, dump_*.json. Helper logic inlines into the scan without persisting, OR (if the script IS the deliverable) mints as a task item with type=scripts and lives under .audit/implementations/<task-id>/<filename>. Complements R-anti-drift-state-dir-allowlist by closing a specific pattern that repeatedly violates it.
rules.last_seen_violation: 2026-04-22 (F006 Antigravity Gemini-3.1-pro-high v1 â€” gen_md.py and gen_state.py written to repo root mid-scan)

rules.canonical_section_current: Â§META-FIXTURE CAPTURE ENVELOPE
rules.gist: Meta-fixtures (F007, F008) emit a capture.json conforming to the canonical envelope: required top-level keys are run_id, fixture, model, ide, audit_md_version, audit_md_short, invocation{temperature,seed,trigger,mode,dry_run}, result, hard_violation_count, soft_violation_count, step_7_5_passed, rules_exercised{}, violations[], soft_violations[], trap_invariants[], verification{}, notes, reviewer, captured_at. Fixture-specific payload (blocks, cross_block_checks, delta detection) lives nested under trap_invariants or verification â€” NEVER promoted to top-level siblings of run_id/fixture/model. audit_md_version MUST be content-hash-verified BEFORE fixture-specific parsing begins. Envelope drift with verified content is a soft violation requiring re-wrap; unverified parse is hard.
rules.last_seen_violation: 2026-04-22 (F007 Antigravity Gemini-3.1-pro-high â€” capture envelope omitted entirely; only {blocks, cross_block_checks, step_7_5_passed} emitted; required canonical re-wrap)
